page: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core processing and memory capabilities. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores, alongside top-tier large-block read memory bandwidth at 338.95 GB/sec. Single-core performance remains in the average tier, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core metrics. Cryptographic and vector operations perform exceptionally well, with PassMark encryption scoring in the top tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare-metal instance offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, though the absence of local NVMe storage requires external storage provisioning. It is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads. It also supports moderate LLM inference, achieving strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium models, though text generation speeds remain average.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server configured with 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores and 1024.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating at 3.5 GHz, this instance features a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory read bandwidth, while single-core metrics sit in the average tier. The server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and high-throughput network applications. Its hardware profile delivers high memory density and strong cryptographic performance, making it suitable for enterprise-scale databases, Redis deployments, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory read performance.

tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring one terabyte of RAM and high-speed networking for demanding enterprise databases and multi-threaded workloads.

1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier large-block read bandwidth
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
